title: Magnetic Resonance Imaging Technologists
description: Operate Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scanners. Monitor patient safety and comfort, and view images of area being scanned to ensure quality of pictures. May administer gadolinium contrast dosage intravenously. May interview patient, explain MRI procedures, and position patient on examining table. May enter into the computer data such as patient history, anatomical area to be scanned, orientation specified, and position of entry.

tasks.text: Review physicians' orders to confirm prescribed exams.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct screening interviews of patients to identify contraindications, such as ferrous objects, pregnancy, prosthetic heart valves, cardiac pacemakers, or tattoos.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select appropriate imaging techniques or coils to produce required images.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanners.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide headphones or earplugs to patients to improve comfort and reduce unpleasant noise.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Position patients on cradle, attaching immobilization devices, if needed, to ensure appropriate placement for imaging.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Take brief medical histories from patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect images for quality, using magnetic resonance scanner equipment and laser camera.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Intravenously inject contrast dyes, such as gadolinium contrast, in accordance with scope of practice.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) equipment to ensure proper functioning and performance in accordance with specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create backup copies of images by transferring images from disk to storage media or workstation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Instruct medical staff or students in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) procedures or equipment operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write reports or notes to summarize testing procedures or outcomes for physicians or other medical professionals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Comfort patients during exams, or request sedatives or other medication from physicians for patients with anxiety or claustrophobia.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Explain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) procedures to patients, patient representatives, or family members.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Calibrate magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) console or peripheral hardware.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Troubleshoot technical issues related to magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanner or peripheral equipment, such as monitors or coils.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Connect physiological leads to physiological acquisition control (PAC) units.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate optical systems to capture dynamic magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images, such as functional brain imaging, real-time organ motion tracking, or musculoskeletal anatomy and trajectory visualization.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach physiological monitoring leads to patient's finger, chest, waist, or other body parts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct inventories to maintain stock of clinical supplies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Place and secure small, portable magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanners on body part to be imaged, such as arm, leg, or head.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop or otherwise produce film records of magnetic resonance images.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Schedule appointments for research subjects or clinical patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
